Ollama overview
Note: You should have at least 8 GB of RAM available to run the 7B models, 16
GB to run the 13B models, and 32 GB to run the 33B models.
If you have GPU, VRAM then will serve for faster LLM response.
What can Ollama do?
Self-hosted a lot of "top" opensource LLM, including LLAMA2 (by Facebook),
Mistral, Phi (from Microsoft), Gemma (by Google), … You can see the full list
here

Support self-hosted opensource embedding model (eg. nomic), and vision
model (eg. LLaVA).

question: What does the term 'Modelfile' refer to in the context of Ollama?
expected_answer: A feature to help people share their model to Ollama library and customize deployed models.
context: Ollama have feature named 'Modelfile' to help people share their model to Ollama library.
chunk_text:
Advanced deployment
Ollama have feature named "Modelfile" to help people share their model to
Ollama libray. This also a feature allow you customize your deployed model.
Using Modelfile, you have the capability to inherit an existing Large Language
Model (LLM) while customizing parameters such as prompt, seed, temperature,
and more.
Example:
In my case using LLM for RAG Chatbot, I want the LLM:
Return the same answer on every request when the input not change, its
mean I need to set a fix seed.
More focused and deterministic, donʼt want the model creative, its mean I
need to reduce the temperature.
So, I create a Modelfile localy with the following content:
The other values in the Modelfile aboce are copy from the original Gemma
Modelfile in the Ollama library.
# Modelfile generated by "ollama show"# Modelfile generated by "ollama show"
# To build a new Modelfile based on this one, replace t# To build a new Modelfile based on this one, replace t
FROM gemma:latestFROM gemma:latest
TEMPLATE TEMPLATE """"""<<start_of_turnstart_of_turn>>useruser
{{{{  ifif .System  .System }}}}{{{{ .System  .System }}}}  {{{{ end  end }}}}{{{{ .Prompt  .Prompt }}}}<<enen
<<start_of_turnstart_of_turn>>modelmodel
{{{{ .Response  .Response }}}}<<end_of_turnend_of_turn>>
""""""
PARAMETER stop PARAMETER stop "<start_of_turn>""<start_of_turn>"
PARAMETER stop PARAMETER stop "<end_of_turn>""<end_of_turn>"
# my changes# my changes
PARAMETER temperature PARAMETER temperature 0.00.0
PARAMETER seed PARAMETER seed 19961996

question: According to the document, what are the requirements for deploying a model using a command line on Linux/MacOS?
expected_answer: You need to follow the instructions for using the command line for deploying a model on Linux/MacOS.
context: Use command line instructions for deploying a model on Linux/MacOS.
chunk_text:
Install GUI app/web (Optional): For who want to have an UI to interaction
(website or application), you need to install additional community app, pick one
you like here.
Deploy a model
This section is talk about my experiment on Linux/MacOS, both using command
line to deploy a model. Not sure Windows the same (using PowerShell/CMD) or
have a better UI management.
Simple deployment

Ollama overview
Note: You should have at least 8 GB of RAM available to run the 7B models, 16
GB to run the 13B models, and 32 GB to run the 33B models.
If you have GPU, VRAM then will serve for faster LLM response.
What can Ollama do?
Self-hosted a lot of "top" opensource LLM, including LLAMA2 (by Facebook),
Mistral, Phi (from Microsoft), Gemma (by Google), … You can see the full list
here
Multi-platform support: Windows (preview), MacOS, and Linux.
Support self-hosted opensource embedding model (eg. nomic), and vision
model (eg. LLaVA).

question: How can you customize a deployed model in Ollama?
expected_answer: You can customize a deployed model in Ollama using a Modelfile with parameters like prompt, seed, and temperature.
context: Using Modelfile, you have the capability to inherit an existing Large Language Model (LLM) while customizing parameters...
chunk_text:
Advanced deployment
Ollama have feature named "Modelfile" to help people share their model to
Ollama libray. This also a feature allow you customize your deployed model.
Using Modelfile, you have the capability to inherit an existing Large Language
Model (LLM) while customizing parameters such as prompt, seed, temperature,
and more.
Example:
In my case using LLM for RAG Chatbot, I want the LLM:
Return the same answer on every request when the input not change, its
mean I need to set a fix seed.
More focused and deterministic, donʼt want the model creative, its mean I
need to reduce the temperature.
So, I create a Modelfile localy with the following content:
The other values in the Modelfile aboce are copy from the original Gemma
Modelfile in the Ollama library.
# Modelfile generated by "ollama show"# Modelfile generated by "ollama show"
# To build a new Modelfile based on this one, replace t# To build a new Modelfile based on this one, replace t
FROM gemma:latestFROM gemma:latest
TEMPLATE TEMPLATE """"""<<start_of_turnstart_of_turn>>useruser
{{{{  ifif .System  .System }}}}{{{{ .System  .System }}}}  {{{{ end  end }}}}{{{{ .Prompt  .Prompt }}}}<<enen
<<start_of_turnstart_of_turn>>modelmodel
{{{{ .Response  .Response }}}}<<end_of_turnend_of_turn>>
""""""
PARAMETER stop PARAMETER stop "<start_of_turn>""<start_of_turn>"
PARAMETER stop PARAMETER stop "<end_of_turn>""<end_of_turn>"
# my changes# my changes
PARAMETER temperature PARAMETER temperature 0.00.0
PARAMETER seed PARAMETER seed 19961996

1515
COPY
